# teletty

> A full terminal in your pocket, via Telegram. Open source Telegram Mini App with smart buttons, Claude Code integration, voice input, and multi-tab tmux sessions.

## What is teletty?

teletty is an open-source Telegram Mini App that gives you full terminal access to any server from your phone. It connects via WebSocket to a Node.js server running tmux, rendering a real terminal in your browser via xterm.js. The killer feature is smart buttons: teletty detects interactive prompts (Y/n, Allow/Deny, numbered options) and renders one-tap buttons, eliminating the need to type on a phone keyboard.

## Key Features

- Smart Buttons: Auto-detects 7 types of interactive prompts and renders clickable buttons
- Claude Code Integration: Built-in support for Claude Code CLI approve/deny prompts with auto-approve mode
- Multi-Tab Sessions: Up to 4 concurrent tmux sessions that survive disconnects
- Voice Input: Push-to-talk via Web Speech API with Azure Whisper fallback
- Telegram-Native Auth: HMAC-SHA256 verification, user ID whitelist, IP-bound JWT sessions
- Emergency Management API: Execute commands via HTTPS when SSH is down
- Mobile Controls: Buttons for arrow keys, Tab, Esc, Ctrl+C
- Tokyo Night Theme: Dark theme optimized for mobile screens

## Technical Details

- Stack: Node.js 18+, Express, ws (WebSocket), node-pty, xterm.js 5.5.0, tmux
- Total codebase: 889 lines across 7 files
- Zero frameworks, zero build step, zero transpilation
- Security: Telegram HMAC-SHA256, timing-safe comparisons, sanitized PTY env, CSP headers, rate limiting
- Deployment: npm, Docker, or manual. Supports Caddy/nginx for HTTPS.

## Installation

npm: npx teletty init && npx teletty
Docker: docker compose up -d
Manual: git clone, npm install, node server.js

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/teletty/teletty
- License: MIT
- Author: Oleg Chetrean
